business.headline: Swiggy Instamart receives 9 notices from food regulator over quality complaintsFSSAI has issued nine notices to Swiggy Instamart over consumer complaints about expired, spoiled and contaminated food deliveries. The regulator has sought detailed compliance records and warned of legal action if the response is delayed.

Apple sues OpenAI, ex-employees over alleged trade secrets theftApple has sued OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misuse of trade secrets for consumer hardware. The case sharpens tensions between partners as both companies push deeper into AI devices.

RBI auctions Rs 32,000 crore in govt bonds: Are they among the safest investments?Government bonds are back in the spotlight after the RBI's latest Rs 32,000 crore auction. What makes them different from other investments, and who are they best suited for? Have a look.

8th Pay Commission: Why its impact goes far beyond government salariesEconomists say the 8th Pay Commission will affect far more than central government salaries and pensions. Its recommendations could shape consumer demand, state finances, inflation and fiscal planning.

File Early, Stay Relaxed: Why the Income Tax Department is sending SMS alertsThousands of taxpayers are receiving SMS reminders from the Income Tax Department asking them to file their ITR early. What's behind these messages?

Sensex ends 828 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; RIL up 2%The BSE Sensex surged 827.57 points, or 1.08%, to close at 77,569.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 244.10 points, or 1.02%, to settle at 24,206.90.

Why wait for the IPO? Some investors are buying shares much earlier. You can tooLong before an IPO opens, and sometimes even years before a company files its draft papers with the market regulator, its shares can already be bought and sold legally in what's known as the unlisted or pre-IPO market.

Income tax department launches ITR-7 Excel utility for AY 2026-27With the utility now live, eligible taxpayers can download it, fill in the required information offline, validate the details, generate a JSON file and upload it on the Income Tax e-Filing portal to complete the return filing process.

TCS's hiring surprise: Why India's biggest IT firm is adding jobs againTCS added 9,279 employees in the April-June quarter after steep headcount declines in late 2025. The shift reflects selective hiring, redeployment and rising demand for AI and digital skills.

Govt eyes stronger forex reserves; FM likely to meet banks soonThe government's move comes at a time when India's foreign exchange reserves have declined sharply. The reserves fell from around $728 billion in February to nearly $682 billion by the end of May.

Home loan on a rented house? Here's what you can still claim under new tax regimeAs taxpayers file their ITRs, understanding home loan tax rules has become more important than ever. In this edition of 'KNOW YOUR TAX', we explain what you can still claim on a rented property under the new tax regime.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech rise up to 4%: Why are IT stocks rising today?TCS's stronger-than-expected June-quarter results pushed IT stocks higher on Friday. The Nifty IT index climbed 1.82%, while the BSE Focused IT index gained nearly 2%.

Sensex jumps 800 points: Why is stock market rising today?Around 10 am, the BSE Sensex was trading 800.61 points, or 1.04%, higher at 77,542.43, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 247.80 points, or 1.03%, to 24,210.60.

Sensex, Nifty open in green lifted by IT stocks as TCS earnings cheer marketsThe BSE Sensex opened at 77,395.63 and climbed 676.12 points, or 0.88%, to 77,417.94 in early trade. The NSE Nifty50 rose 196.30 points, or 0.82%, to 24,159.10. The benchmarks had also ended higher in the previous session after recovering from Wednesday's sharp sell-off.

Why TCS may have to rethink its dividend to win the AI raceTCS is winning more AI work, but analysts say scaling it will need heavier investment. That could push the company to rebalance dividends and future growth spending.

EPF Scheme 2026: What it is, who benefits and what it means for youFrom simpler withdrawal rules to new safeguards for retirement savings, the EPF Scheme, 2026 brings several important changes. Here's a quick look at who stands to benefit and what the new rules mean for you.

Tax regime to e-verify: Income Tax Department's 7 golden rules for ITR filingThe Income Tax Department has issued seven golden rules for filing ITRs for AY 2026-27. The checklist urges early preparation, careful verification and timely e-verification to avoid delays and lost benefits.

TCS adds over 9,200 employees in Q1, logs biggest hiring in over a yearAlongside higher hiring, TCS reported a solid financial performance for the June quarter. Consolidated net profit stood at Rs 13,349 crore, while revenue rose 14% year-on-year to Rs 72,275 crore.

TCS Q1 results: Revenue jumps 14%; IT major declares Rs 12 interim dividendThe company posted double-digit growth in both revenue and profit, helped by steady demand from clients, especially in the banking and financial services sector. It also announced an interim dividend of Rs 12 per share for its shareholders.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite US-Iran tensions; TCS results in focusBenchmark indices closed higher on Thursday as investors returned to beaten-down stocks after Wednesday's sharp sell-off. The rebound was led by midcaps and realty shares, while Middle East tensions, crude prices and earnings remained in focus.

entertainment.headline: The Odyssey Mumbai screening: Christopher Nolan, Tom Holland, Matt Damon grab chai Christopher Nolan, Matt Damon and Tom Holland stopped for chai at a Mumbai cafe before The Odyssey premiere. The brief visit, added a local touch ahead of the red-carpet screening.

Lenin box office day 1: Akhil Akkineni's film delivers his career-best opening Actor Akhil Akkineni's Lenin opened in theatres on July 10 and witnessed a strong first day at the box office. The makers claimed a career-best opening, though trade estimates differ in terms of worldwide total.

Akansha Ranjan, Sharan Sharma marry in intimate ceremony; Alia Bhatt leads wishes Akansha Ranjan married filmmaker Sharan Sharma in an intimate Mumbai ceremony on July 11. The wedding drew warm wishes from film friends, including actors Alia Bhatt and Ananya Panday.

Singer S Janaki dies; CM Vijay, Rajinikanth mourn Nightingale of South India Veteran playback singer S Janaki, known as Nightingale of South India, died at the age of 88 in Mysore. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay and actors Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan and Chiranjeevi paid tribute to the late singer.

After ZEE5 takedown, Satluj spreads across Punjab villages via private screenings After the government ordered Satluj off ZEE5 in India, villagers in Punjab began holding private screenings. The film's spread through pen drives and peer networks has turned the takedown into a wider debate on memory, censorship and politics.

Jana Nayagan coming soon, says CM Vijay after film's A rating Actor Vijay has confirmed Jana Nayagan's censor certification and is headed for its theatrical release. The announcement follows a prolonged dispute with the censor board resolved after 12 suggested modifications.

Why Christopher Nolan chose Mumbai? Real reason behind The Odyssey India premiere Director Christopher Nolan has opened up on the real reason behind holding The Odyssey's India premiere in Mumbai, linking the visit to his previous experience of the city's vibrant film culture and audiences.

I'd give my right arm: Shekhar Kapur to Matt Damon after he recalls missing his film Filmmaker Shekhar Kapur has warmly responded to Matt Damon's wish to collaborate with him. He praised the Oscar-winning actor's emotional depth and says it is finally time for them to work together.

Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj removed from ZEE5 International after India takedown Actor Diljit Dosanjh's film, Satluj has been removed from ZEE5's international library days after its removal in India. The latest move deepens the film's long-running certification and release controversy.

TV actor Rohit Chandel arrested for allegedly stalking and harassing a minor Mumbai Police arrested television actor Rohit Chandel after a 16-year-old girl accused him of alleged stalking, molestation and assault. He has been booked under the POCSO Act and BNS sections, with the investigation continuing.

Matt Damon wants to work with Shekhar Kapur after missing The Four Feathers At a Mumbai press conference for The Odyssey, actor Matt Damon said he wants to work with Shekhar Kapur. He recalled missing The Four Feathers years ago and said the wish has stayed with him.

Ramayana trailer: Ranbir Kapoor-Yash epic to get a grand launch on July 24 The makers of Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana have confirmed that the film's trailer will be unveiled on July 24. Starring Ranbir Kapoor, Yash, Sunny Deol and Sai Pallavi in key roles, the much-awaited mythological epic is set to kick off its promotional campaign with the grand launch.

Bring Matt Damon home: How The Odyssey keeps Hollywood's $600 million joke alive The old internet gag about Hollywood repeatedly rescuing actor Matt Damon finds new life with The Odyssey. This time, Damon's next great homecoming depends on nobody but himself: a quiet reminder that every great journey, on screen or off, is ultimately about finding your way back. And honestly? We'll still be cheering from the shore.

Dhamaal 4 box office Day 1: Ajay Devgn film opens big, misses Total Dhamaal record Actor Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 opened strongly on July 10, beating early trade estimates on day 1. The comedy earned Rs 13.75 crore on the opening day.

Alia Bhatt stuns in purple saree, dances at bestie Akansha Ranjan's pre-wedding bash Actor Alia Bhatt grooved to best friend Akansha Ranjan Kapoor and Sharan Sharma's pre-wedding festivities in Mumbai. Several videos from the pre-wedding bash have emerged online.

Boney Kapoor expresses gratitude for PM Modi's letter blessing Anshula-Rohan Boney Kapoor shared Prime Minister Narendra Modi's congratulatory letter for Anshula Kapoor and Rohan Thakkar after their wedding. The filmmaker wrote how the letter added warmth to the family's intimate celebrations.

Vicky Donor, Badhaai Ho inspired me to do Super Subbu: Sundeep Kishan Actor Sundeep Kishan said Vicky Donor and Badhaai Ho inspired him to headline Super Subbu. He said the Telugu series uses humour and sensitivity to discuss sex education without sensationalism.

Thamizh Murugan: Dhanush rides an elephant, wields a vel in striking first look Actor Dhanush's Thamizh Murugan first look is out and marks his reunion with director Vetri Maaran for their fifth film together. The poster's powerful imagery has fuelled discussion around its cultural roots and the Lord Murugan connection.

Christopher Nolan thrilled to launch The Odyssey in India, thanks fans. Watch Filmmaker Christopher Nolan opened The Odyssey in Mumbai with actors Tom Holland and Matt Damon and thanked the audience for its response. The launch marked Nolan's first film premiere in the city, placing India among the earliest stops on the global premiere tour.

general news.headline: NOR 1-1 ENG live: England hunt for last gasp winnerNorway vs England live, FIFA World Cup quarterfinal: (NOR 1-1 ENG) Norway and England are trading punches in a thrilling World Cup quarterfinal, with both sides pushing relentlessly for a late winner. Norway rattled the crossbar before England hit back, setting up a tense finale with just 10 minutes of normal time remaining.

Iran closes Hormuz after firing warning shot at vessel: State mediaIran's IRGC Navy said it has temporarily closed the Strait of Hormuz until further notice after firing warning shots at a vessel it accused of using an unapproved route, according to state media.

How a Vietnam island-hopping tour ended in tragedy for 15 IndiansVietnam Boat Accident: The speedboat overturned about 400 metres off Hon May Rut Ngoai, near Phu Quoc Island, while it was carrying tourists from Hon May Rut Islet to An Thoi Port, according to Vietnamese news outlet VNExpress.

Singer S Janaki dies; CM Vijay, Rajinikanth mourn Nightingale of South IndiaVeteran playback singer S Janaki, known as Nightingale of South India, died at the age of 88 in Mysore. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay and actors Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan and Chiranjeevi paid tribute to the late singer.

Cops say Siya killed Ketan, proving it will be easier said than donePune Police has alleged that Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary pushed Ketan Agarwal off Lohagad Fort. The case now rests on whether investigators can prove an unbroken chain of circumstantial evidence.

I am not Gandhi, be your own hero: Sonam Wangchuk urges citizens to join protestSonam Wangchuk, on the 14th day of his hunger strike, said he was "just an ordinary citizen" and called on people to actively join the movement over examination irregularities.

Indian-origin Google techie, 57, killed by husband after dispute in USKirk B Wrzesien has been charged with murder, two counts of aggravated assault and two counts of illegally possessing a firearm while committing a crime.

Linda Noskova outlasts Karolina Muchova to clinch maiden Wimbledon titleLinda Noskova defeated Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 in a thrilling all-Czech final to win her maiden Grand Slam title and become the youngest Wimbledon champion since Petra Kvitova in 2011.

What does the soundtrack in Satluj's CM assassination scene really mean?The film's use of a hymn from Sri Guru Granth Sahib invites a wider discussion about context, interpretation and a practice that has become increasingly familiar in Sikh public life.

Army chief Asim Munir has new job in PakistanPakistan has placed army chief Asim Munir on a committee to address rapid population growth. The move comes as Asim Munir fights threats from Afghanistan and also works to mediate between the US and Iran.

India lose No.1 T20I ranking after UK tour ends with a stinker in SouthamptonIndia return from the tour of UK without winning a single match. First a 0-2 series loss against Ireland, and then an abject surrender of 0-4 against England, this is one of the lowest moments of the Indian cricket team in the past few years.

10 from Tamil Nadu, 5 from Andhra, Kerala: Vietnam tragedy kills 15 south IndiansA leisure trip to Vietnam's Phu Quoc Island ended in tragedy after a speedboat carrying Indian tourists capsized, killing 15 people — all from Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh and Kerala. As the scale of the tragedy emerged, governments in the three southern states stepped in to assist families and coordinate relief efforts.
The Panipat route: How clothes from around the world reach Sarojini"Sarojini ke kapde pehen ke jaati madam disco." But where do those clothes actually come from? We went to Panipat to find out.

Hyderabad student missing in Finland found dead in Helsinki sea, family seeks probeThe body of Hyderabad student Manideep Reddy, missing since May 5, has been recovered from the sea in Helsinki. His family has called the death suspicious and demanded a proper investigation by Finnish authorities.

Heatwave to make a comeback on Sunday. Rain in Uttarakhand, Bihar, AssamThe monsoon is set to stay active across large parts of India on July 12, with the IMD warning of heavy rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds in several states.

Minerva Academy stun defending champions to lift Helsinki Cup in FinlandMinerva Academy FC completed their redemption story by defeating defending champions HJK 1-0 in the final to lift the 2026 Helsinki Cup. The Chandigarh-based academy finally won the only major European youth title that had eluded them after finishing runners-up in Finland last year.

Monsoon gone again? Satellite captures 70% of India without rain cloudsThe monsoon has changed across India, leaving vast regions with little rainfall. Something thousands of kilometres away might be disrupting the rains.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

BJP offered my party MLA Rs 20-30 crore: Omar Abdullah's 'Operation Lotus' chargeMeanwhile, Chief Minister Omar Abdullah attacked the BJP-led Centre and asked it not to mistake his patience for weakness on the restoration of statehood for Jammu and Kashmir, and demanded clarity on what it meant by the "appropriate time" for taking that step.

South Africa footballer, 25, dies days after featuring in FIFA World CupSouth Africa international Jayden Adams has died at the age of 25, just weeks after representing his country at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Adams featured in all three group-stage matches as South Africa reached the knockout rounds for the first time in their history.
India's warship building worriesA burst of inductions but concerns about a gap in orders and indigenous technology

5 women die in 6 days after C-section, Rajasthan hospital under scannerA positive operation theatre culture report has raised infection control concerns, but authorities deny any link to the deaths, maintaining the women died from medical complications rather than infection.

Mojtaba vows payback for father Khamenei's death, says vengeance is nation's demandAyatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, in his first public message since succeeding his father, vowed to avenge Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's killing, calling it "the demand of the nation", even as tensions between Iran and the US continue despite ongoing diplomatic talks.

Sabrina Pasterski built a plane at 12, rejected NASA, now called the next EinsteinSabrina Gonzalez Pasterski built and flew an aircraft at the age of 14, graduated from MIT with a perfect GPA in three years, had her research cited by Stephen Hawking, and turned down offers from NASA, Blue Origin and a reported $1.1 million academic position to pursue some of physics' biggest unanswered questions.

sports.headline: India Women within touching distance of Lord's Test triumph after commanding Day 2 Kranti Gaud claimed her maiden five-wicket haul in red-ball cricket before Smriti Mandhana's unbeaten 69 helped India Women stretch their lead to 269 against England, leaving the visitors in complete control after Day 2 of the Lord's Test.

NOR 1-1 ENG live: Norway put England under the pump with 10 minutes to go Norway vs England live, FIFA World Cup quarterfinal: (NOR 1-1 ENG) One hour gone in Miami as Norway and England remain locked in a tense battle after VAR drama denies the Norwegians a second goal.

Shreyas Iyer faces the media, explains Vaibhav Sooryavanshi snub after poor UK tour ENG vs IND, 5th T20I: Shreyas Iyer defended India's decision to drop Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for the final T20I against England, saying the team wanted to test a different opening combination. The India captain also reflected on the team's disastrous 4-0 UK tour and his captaincy.

Linda Noskova cries tears of joy, dedicates title to mother who died from cancer Linda Noskova broke down in tears after defeating Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 to win her maiden Wimbledon title, dedicating the triumph to her mother, Ivana, who died after a battle with cancer in 2024.

India lose No.1 T20I ranking after UK tour ends with a stinker in Southampton India return from the tour of UK without winning a single match. First a 0-2 series loss against Ireland, and then an abject surrender of 0-4 against England, this is one of the lowest moments of the Indian cricket team in the past few years.

Linda Noskova outlasts Karolina Muchova to clinch maiden Wimbledon title Linda Noskova defeated Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 in a thrilling all-Czech final to win her maiden Grand Slam title and become the youngest Wimbledon champion since Petra Kvitova in 2011.

Sacked once before, India fielding coach unlikely to get another extension India fielding coach T Dilip is unlikely to receive a contract extension after the ongoing white-ball tour of England, India Today has learnt. While the BCCI is expected to retain Morne Morkel and Ryan ten Doeschate, Dilip's second stint with the national team appears to be nearing its end.

Record partnership, highest total: Buttler, Brook add insult to India's injury Jos Buttler and Harry Brook smashed a record 233-run opening partnership as England posted 257/3 against India in the fifth T20I at Southampton. The total is England's highest-ever against India in T20Is and leaves the visitors staring at the possibility of losing their No. 1 ICC ranking.

South Africa footballer, 25, dies days after featuring in FIFA World Cup South Africa international Jayden Adams has died at the age of 25, just weeks after representing his country at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Adams featured in all three group-stage matches as South Africa reached the knockout rounds for the first time in their history.

Rusty Argentina seek inspiration beyond Lionel Messi in quarter-final clash FIFA World Cup, Argentina vs Switzerland preview: Lionel Messi was left in tears after Argentina just about scrapped through to the quarter-final of their tournament. The rusty defending champions will have to pull out all their tricks to beat a disciplined Switzerland side on Sunday, July 12.

Kranti Gaud rocks England at Lord's, becomes first woman on iconic Honours Board Kranti Gaud etched her name into Lord's history on Saturday, becoming the first woman to feature on the iconic Test Honours Board after claiming a maiden five-wicket haul in Test cricket.

Sunil Gavaskar blasts India after team drops Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from 5th T20ILegendary Sunil Gavaskar blasted the Indian team management after the visiting team dropped Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from their playing XI in their final T20I of the England series. Gavaskar said that he was very disappointed with coaching staff's call.

Final Highlights: Linda Noskova beats Karolina Muchova, wins maiden SlamWimbledon Final Live Updates: Linda Noskova has won her maiden Grand Slam title after beating Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 in the women's singles final. Loskova dropped five championship points in the second set, but managed to finish it off clinically.

Minerva Academy stun defending champions to lift Helsinki Cup in FinlandMinerva Academy FC completed their redemption story by defeating defending champions HJK 1-0 in the final to lift the 2026 Helsinki Cup. The Chandigarh-based academy finally won the only major European youth title that had eluded them after finishing runners-up in Finland last year.

5 matches, 14 overs: Harshit Rana ruled out of India vs England ODI seriesHarshit Rana has been ruled out of India's three-match ODI series against England. Rana recently made his return to the Indian team after a 4-month injury layoff. He bowled just 14 overs in 5 T20Is since his return to the Indian team.

Toss in Southampton delayed after India team gets stuck in traffic jamIND vs ENG, 5th T20I: The toss in Southampton was delayed after the Indian team got caught in a traffic jam on their way to the Rose Bowl. India will be looking to salvage some pride after already conceding the series to England.

Ramiz Raja defends Shan Masood: Can't expect miracles from ordinary playersRamiz Raja defended Shan Masood following his removal as Pakistan's Test captain, arguing that no skipper can be expected to deliver results with an underperforming side.

Shubman Gill swaps bat for racket, picks Carlos Alcaraz as dream doubles partnerShubman Gill picked Carlos Alcaraz as his dream doubles partner during his visit to Wimbledon, saying the Spaniard's energy on court would make for a fun pairing and backing himself to hold his own in tennis.

No penalties for Messi? Argentina coach opens up before Swiss quarterfinal testFIFA World Cup: Lionel Scaloni said Lionel Messi will decide whether he takes Argentina's next penalty against Switzerland. The leading goal-scorer has missed two penalties from the spot in this tournament.

Indian football team to tour New Zealand for two-match friendly seriesThe Indian men's football team will travel to New Zealand for a historic two-match friendly series during the November 2026 FIFA international window, with the fixtures forming part of celebrations marking 100 years of sporting ties between the two nations.

Norway vs England: Haaland's Vikings stand between England and semisFIFA World Cup, quarter-final: Erling Haaland vs Harry Kane. Norway vs England. Two in-form teams, fresh off impressive Round of 16 victories, now collide in Miami with a place in the semifinals on the line.

Scaloni fires back over FIFA favouritism claims: Some don't want Argentina to winFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Scaloni rejected claims FIFA is favouring Argentina after the controversial comeback win over Egypt. He said resentment towards the champions is motivating his squad before the quarter-final against Switzerland.

Virat Kohli's privacy invaded in London? England star recalls bizarre cafe incidentJordan Cox said Virat Kohli was unknowingly photographed during a private London cafe meeting before the former's Test debut earlier this year. It highlighted the former India captain's difficulty living normally despite staying low-profile.

India in desperate search for answers with No.1 T20I ranking on the lineIND vs ENG: India head into the fifth T20I in Southampton under immense pressure amid a disastrous tour of England, with Shreyas Iyer's side still searching for their first win and facing the prospect of losing their No.1 ranking.

England captain Harry Kane confirms golf outing with US president Donald TrumpEngland captain Harry Kane has confirmed he played a round of golf with U.S. President Donald Trump in Florida around 18 months ago, calling it a "surreal" experience and saying the president is a good golfer.

The blessing and curse of being Novak Djokovic at 39Novak Djokovic beat time in Melbourne, then outlasted Felix Auger-Aliassime at Wimbledon. Jannik Sinner ended it in straight sets. At 39, one match from a 25th major, he is living the strange arithmetic of a career that refuses to let him rest.

Animesh Kujur clocks 10.14s, records fastest 100m by an Indian on foreign soilIndia's sprint sensation Animesh Kujur continued his rapid rise by clocking a lifetime best of 10.14 seconds to finish second in the men's 100m final at the PUMA Fast Arms Legs 2026 meet, setting the fastest 100m ever run by an Indian outside the country.

Belgium's Courtois backs Lammens to recover from costly World Cup blunderBelgium goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois threw his support behind Senne Lammens after the young substitute's late mistake proved decisive in Belgium's 2-1 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup quarter-final on Friday.

Stokes retirement video: ICC closes anti-corruption enquiry with no action vs ECBThe England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) will face no action from the International Cricket Council (ICC) after the governing body concluded its enquiry into the release of Ben Stokes' retirement video.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
